nameMeanings.name: Beddingfield
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Suffolk): habitational name from Bedingfield in Suffolk. The placename is recorded in Domesday Book as Bedingefelda, from the Old English personal name Bēda + the connective particle -ing- ‘associated with, named after’ + feld ‘open country’.

variants.name: Bedingfield
meanings: English (Suffolk and Norfolk): variant of   Beddingfield  .

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Henry Beddingfield was born in 1401, in St Cleer, Cornwall, England as the son of Sir Thomas Beddingfield. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Emma Batchelor. He died in 1479, in St Cleer, Cornwall, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 78.

ja.St Cleer, Cornwall, England: イギリスイギリスコルンワルスト クリール

ko.lifeSummary: Henry Beddingfield(은)는 1401년 영국 코른월 스트 클리어에서 Sir Thomas Beddingfield의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Emma Batchelor(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1479년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월 스트 클리어에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.
